QR Code Generators Compared

The QR generator market is full of "free" tools with expiring trials and watermarks. This table compares what you actually get.

Feature QRMint qr-code-generator.com QR Code Monkey Uniqode
Static codes free forever Yes — never expire Trial-based; dynamic codes expire after 14 days Yes No — subscription required
Sign-up required No Yes, for most features No (account for saved codes) Yes
Logo embedding Free Paid plans Free Paid plans
Custom colors Free Limited free Free Paid plans
SVG / vector export Free Paid plans Free (with account) Paid plans
Watermark on free tier Never On some outputs No N/A (no real free tier)
Privacy (runs in browser) Yes — data never uploaded No — server-generated No — server-generated No — server-generated
WiFi QR codes Free Free Free Paid
vCard contact codes Free Dynamic only (expires) Free Paid
Dynamic codes (edit + tracking) Premium Paid plans from ~$5/mo Via paid QRCode Studio Paid plans from ~$5/mo

When QRMint is the right choice

You want a QR code that works forever on printed material, with your logo and colors, without creating an account or worrying about a trial expiring. Privacy matters — WiFi passwords and contacts are encoded locally, never uploaded.

When a paid platform makes sense

You run large campaigns and need to retarget destinations after printing, A/B test landing pages, or report scan analytics to a client. That requires dynamic codes — see our Premium plan or an enterprise platform like Uniqode.

Comparison based on publicly listed features and pricing as of July 2026. Details may change — check each provider's site before committing.
